page: Microsoft Azure E128-64ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-family virtual machine configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, powered by AMD processors. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU.

The instance features 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-throughput external storage volumes. It does not include dedicated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the E128-64ads_v7 offers high memory and local storage capacity relative to its compute core count. This profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial local scratch space but do not demand GPU acceleration or specialized CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 or AMX, which are absent in this configuration.

This server configuration is suitable for large-scale relational databases, in-memory analytics, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring high memory capacity and extensive local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure E128-64ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the Eadsv7 family, powered by AMD processors and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs paired with 1024.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include 7559 GB of local disk capacity alongside Premium Storage compatibility for external volume integration. The instance does not include GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource configuration is optimized for memory-intensive workloads and large-scale database deployments that require substantial local storage capacity but do not require GPU acceleration or advanced vector instruction sets. It is suited for in-memory data processing, large databases, and enterprise applications.
